objectives: Explain the First Law of Thermodynamics in terms of internal energy, heat, and work
Derive the mathematical statement ΔU = Q - W and explain each term
Distinguish between heat and temperature at the molecular level
Apply the First Law to an ideal gas undergoing isothermal and adiabatic processes
Describe the sign convention for Q and W in the physics and chemistry conventions

required_claims.statement: The First Law of Thermodynamics states that energy is conserved: ΔU = Q - W
Internal energy U is a state function; Q and W are path-dependent
For an ideal gas, internal energy depends only on temperature
